visa_types.overview:
Overview
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Religious > Visa Fees 1]

All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. The Visa fees must be paid in Cash only. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
Please Note: The service fee is payable per application in addition to the visa fees.
Only the following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees: 
- Child under 6 years
- Diplomatic Passport with Note Verbale for mission purpose
- Religious (For invitation, mission and religious purpose and not for tourism purpose.)
- Dependent parents and Spouse of EU National

## Documents Required
For more details about the documents required click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/whkp8GdhO5SbCsBAno3gG/ce02444ad59a6ca93014ec557302dd73/religious-checklist.pdf)
In any case, according to regulations, the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem, through Visa Application Center may:
- request any other documents, the appearance of the applicant or a personal interview
- should resolve all applications within a maximum period of 3 months
- archive the visa application process if it has not been picked up by the applicant within one month of notification or grant

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/19QIcbB2cKb5uBsupwwgrX/3506b27e853bf9322190c615bbf279cd/New-Application-form-Italy-2025-Apr.pdf)
Authorization Form for Minors (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ySekRrH5SppQXxqq8Veye/8dbd791b62c909ad16514ddbbe00817e/authorization-form-for-minors.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa option is for applicants who wish to study or do research in Italy for no more than 90 days.
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa option is for applicants who wish to study or do research in Italy for no more than 90 days.
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Study > Visa Fees 1]

All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. The Visa fees must be paid in Cash only. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
Please Note: The service fee is payable per application in addition to the visa fees.
Only the following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees: 
- Child under 6 years
- Diplomatic Passport with Note Verbale for mission purpose
- Religious (For invitation, mission and religious purpose and not for tourism purpose.)
- Dependent parents and Spouse of EU National

## Documents Required

In any case, according to regulations, the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem, through Visa Application Center may:
- request any other documents, the appearance of the applicant or a personal interview
- should resolve all applications within a maximum period of 3 months
- archive the visa application process if it has not been picked up by the applicant within one month of notification or grant

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.

## Visa Fees

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]

All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. The Visa fees must be paid in Cash only. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
Please Note: The service fee is payable per application in addition to the visa fees.
Only the following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees: 
- Child under 6 years
- Diplomatic Passport with Note Verbale for mission purpose
- Religious (For invitation, mission and religious purpose and not for tourism purpose.)
- Dependent parents and Spouse of EU National

## Documents Required
For more details about the documents required click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4IzBj3CD23VUG25MR9SEM1/cb212d8b7028cd07998993f4ce7da2cd/business-checklist.pdf)
In any case, according to regulations, the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem, through Visa Application Center may:
- request any other documents, the appearance of the applicant or a personal interview
- should resolve all applications within a maximum period of 3 months
- archive the visa application process if it has not been picked up by the applicant within one month of notification or grant

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/19QIcbB2cKb5uBsupwwgrX/3506b27e853bf9322190c615bbf279cd/New-Application-form-Italy-2025-Apr.pdf)

visa_types.name: Visa Type D – FAMILY REASONS FOR MEMBERS OF EU CITIZENS
visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – FAMILY REASONS FOR MEMBERS OF EU CITIZENS > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The family members who have the right to reunification are exclusively those indicated in the art. 2 of Legislative Decree 30/20007:
- the spouse;
- the partner who has contracted a registered partnership with the EU citizen on the basis of the legislation of a Member State, if the legislation of the Member State host equates such registered partnership to marriage and in compliance with the established conditions by the relevant legislation of the host Member State;
- direct descendants under the age of 21 or dependents and those of the spouse or partner as per letter b);
- direct ascendants and those of the spouse or partner referred to in letter b, if financially dependent.
Adoptees/adopters are also considered as equivalent to the direct descendant/direct ascendant, as well as minors subjected to permanent guardianship/custody pursuant to Law. 184/83.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – FAMILY REASONS FOR MEMBERS OF EU CITIZENS > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
The family members who have the right to reunification are exclusively those indicated in the art. 2 of Legislative Decree 30/20007:
- the spouse;
- the partner who has contracted a registered partnership with the EU citizen on the basis of the legislation of a Member State, if the legislation of the Member State host equates such registered partnership to marriage and in compliance with the established conditions by the relevant legislation of the host Member State;
- direct descendants under the age of 21 or dependents and those of the spouse or partner as per letter b);
- direct ascendants and those of the spouse or partner referred to in letter b, if financially dependent.
Adoptees/adopters are also considered as equivalent to the direct descendant/direct ascendant, as well as minors subjected to permanent guardianship/custody pursuant to Law. 184/83.
The applicant who intends to apply for a visa for family reasons must provide:
- Invitation letter from the Italian/EU family member by which he/she states the intention to exercise the right to family unity, accompanied by a valid identity document;
- Civil status documentation certifying the status of family member pursuant to art. 2 of DL 30/2007, translated and legalized (with an Apostille, when applicable), or confirmation of the registration of marriage or civil union as transcribed in Italy;
- In cases where financial dependency assessment is envisaged: demonstration of economic remittances by the Italian/EU citizen. The financial support must be structural and continuative in its nature (there is no minimum duration) and must allow the beneficiary, in consideration of his/her financial and social conditions in the State of origin or provenance, to provide for his/her essential needs (COM(2009)313 paragraph 2.1.4)
The visa is issued free of charge, and the requests for an appointment to lodge the visa application the request will be treated expeditiously

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/19QIcbB2cKb5uBsupwwgrX/3506b27e853bf9322190c615bbf279cd/New-Application-form-Italy-2025-Apr.pdf)

visa_types.name: Visa Type D – SELF EMPLOYMENT
visa_types.documents_required:
- Entry visa application form
- Recent passport photograph (2)
- Valid travel document with an expiry date at least three months after the expiry date of the visa applied for
- Certificate of the abstract identification of the resources necessary for the entrepreneurial, commercial or craft activity to be carried out, issued by the Chamber of Commerce competent for the area in which the activity is to be carried out. These resources must not be less than three times the annual social security benefit.
- The availability of economic resources in Italy sufficient to guarantee the amount of the necessary resources indicated in the certificate
- The availability of an income - in relation to the previous financial year - higher than the minimum amount provided by law for exemption from contribution to health expenditure (8,500 euro), already obtained in the country of residence.
- Nulla Osta of the territorially competent police headquarters
- Availability of accommodation
visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – SELF EMPLOYMENT > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is for applicants who require work permit (permesso di soggiorno per Self-Employment (Lavoro Autonomo)) given their work requires or permits them to be self-employed.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is for applicants who require work permit (permesso di soggiorno per Self-Employment (Lavoro Autonomo)) given their work requires or permits them to be self-employed.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – SELF EMPLOYMENT > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Entry visa application form
- Recent passport photograph (2)
- Valid travel document with an expiry date at least three months after the expiry date of the visa applied for
- Certificate of the abstract identification of the resources necessary for the entrepreneurial, commercial or craft activity to be carried out, issued by the Chamber of Commerce competent for the area in which the activity is to be carried out. These resources must not be less than three times the annual social security benefit.
- The availability of economic resources in Italy sufficient to guarantee the amount of the necessary resources indicated in the certificate
- The availability of an income - in relation to the previous financial year - higher than the minimum amount provided by law for exemption from contribution to health expenditure (8,500 euro), already obtained in the country of residence.
- Nulla Osta of the territorially competent police headquarters
- Availability of accommodation

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/19QIcbB2cKb5uBsupwwgrX/3506b27e853bf9322190c615bbf279cd/New-Application-form-Italy-2025-Apr.pdf)

visa_types.name: LONG TERM “D” VISA Type
visa_types.overview: A long-term entry visa (for more than 90 days) is called a “National Visa” NV and grants access for a long-term stay in the State that issued it. It allows free circulation for a period of no more than 90 days per half-year in the territory of other Member States.
visa_types.full_text: A long-term entry visa (for more than 90 days) is called a “National Visa” NV and grants access for a long-term stay in the State that issued it. It allows free circulation for a period of no more than 90 days per half-year in the territory of other Member States.

visa_types.name: Short- Term Visa (Maximum stay of 90 days)
visa_types.overview:
If you are planning a short visit to Italy for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Italy is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days during a period of 6 months from the date of the first entry into the territory of the Schengen States. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time.
- Applications are only accepted within 3 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph
- Application will be accepted only until 15 days (excluding VFS handling time) before the date of travel.
- Applicants applying for Schengen Visa have to complete the visa application form and affix recent photograph.
visa_types.full_text:
If you are planning a short visit to Italy for up to 90 days or less and you do not have the nationality of one of the countries of the European Economic Area (EEA), you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Italy is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa for a stay of up to 90 days during a period of 6 months from the date of the first entry into the territory of the Schengen States. Please note that the following essentials must be met by all applicants submitting their applications for visa.
- Passport should have at least two blank pages.
- Passport should be valid for at least 3 months from the date on which the applicant intends to leave the SCHENGEN territory, or, in the case of multiple journeys the date on which the applicant intends to leave it for the last time.
- Applications are only accepted within 3 months of the requested date of arrival
- Relevant Forms & Photograph
- Application will be accepted only until 15 days (excluding VFS handling time) before the date of travel.
- Applicants applying for Schengen Visa have to complete the visa application form and affix recent photograph.

visa_types.name: Visa Type D – STUDY (More than 90 days)
visa_types.documents_required:
- Entry visa application form (type D)
- recent passport-size photograph (1)
- valid travel document whose expiry date is at least three months longer than that of the visa requested
- proof that the applicant has adequate accommodation in Italy for the whole period of study in Italy
- proof that the applicant has sufficient means of subsistence for their whole stay in Italy, for an amount not lower than that specified in Table A annexed to the Directive of the Ministry of the Interior dated 1st March 2000 .This can be proven in one of the following ways: evidence of personal or financial resources; economic guarantees provided by Italian institutions and bodies of an established credit, by local Governments, or by foreign institutions and bodies considered to be reliable by the Italian diplomatic mission
- pre-enrolment registration on the website
https://www.universitaly.it/ (https://www.universitaly.it/)
of the course to be followed in Italy (please note without this we cannot accept the file)
- detailed acceptance letter from the University
- flight reservation
- Medical Insurance valid for Italy for all the medical expenses, hospital recovery etc for the whole duration of the study period
visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – STUDY (More than 90 days) > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

visa_types.name: Events/ Conference/ Workshop
visa_types.documents_required:
For more details about the documents required click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6p2WBlwqlfrFQNOuncfAff/9f750886ef63178356923f38c66f7e81/cultural-events-checklist.pdf)
In any case, according to regulations, the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem, through Visa Application Center may:
- request any other documents, the appearance of the applicant or a personal interview
- should resolve all applications within a maximum period of 3 months
- archive the visa application process if it has not been picked up by the applicant within one month of notification or grant
visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > pse > en > Table:Events/ Conference/ Workshop > Visa Fees 1]

All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. The Visa fees must be paid in Cash only. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
Please Note: The service fee is payable per application in addition to the visa fees.
Only the following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees: 
- Child under 6 years
- Diplomatic Passport with Note Verbale for mission purpose
- Religious (For invitation, mission and religious purpose and not for tourism purpose.)
- Dependent parents and Spouse of EU National

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Only Nationals of Palestinian Territories and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Palestinian Territories can apply for a visa at the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy/Consulate of the country in which the traveller is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveller is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy/Consulate of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Events/ Conference/ Workshop > Visa Fees 1]

All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate. The Visa fees must be paid in Cash only. It is subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
Please Note: The service fee is payable per application in addition to the visa fees.
Only the following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees: 
- Child under 6 years
- Diplomatic Passport with Note Verbale for mission purpose
- Religious (For invitation, mission and religious purpose and not for tourism purpose.)
- Dependent parents and Spouse of EU National

## Documents Required
For more details about the documents required click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6p2WBlwqlfrFQNOuncfAff/9f750886ef63178356923f38c66f7e81/cultural-events-checklist.pdf)
In any case, according to regulations, the Consulate General of Italy in Jerusalem, through Visa Application Center may:
- request any other documents, the appearance of the applicant or a personal interview
- should resolve all applications within a maximum period of 3 months
- archive the visa application process if it has not been picked up by the applicant within one month of notification or grant

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/19QIcbB2cKb5uBsupwwgrX/3506b27e853bf9322190c615bbf279cd/New-Application-form-Italy-2025-Apr.pdf)
Authorization Form for Minors (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ySekRrH5SppQXxqq8Veye/8dbd791b62c909ad16514ddbbe00817e/authorization-form-for-minors.pdf) 
Cultural and Events Invitation-Letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/38HH0Gzb5BQHfdak1lKoDJ/85dd42c28f94d554e33faaecfafdad7b/cultural-events-invitation-letter.pdf)

visa_types.name: Visa Type D – Digital Nomad
visa_types.documents_required:
- Passport in original
- Identity pictures.(2) – passport size
- Your study documents (degree, professional status etc) – you need to demonstrate to be an high professional operator (please check art 27 quarter comma 1 dlg 286/98)
- Proof of health insurance for at least 1 year valid for Italy
- Proof of financial income (its source, the amount, etc.).
- Evidence of remote work (clients, work contracts, business license) –
- Civil documents (birth certificate, CV, qualifications, etc.)
- Proof of accommodation (at least 1 year)
- Proof of previous experience in the field (at least six months)
All the documents have to be in original , translated and apostilled

visa_types.name: Visa Type D – Family Reunion
visa_types.documents_required:
- Application form visa (type D) duly completed and signed by the visa applicant
- Recent photograph in passport size (2 per person)
- Valid passport or travel document (original plus one photocopy), valid at least 3 months and with at least two blank pages. The passport will be kept and returned with the visa, if approved;
- Nulla Osta” (Entry Clearance) issued no more than 6 months earlier by the competent “Sportello Unico” (Unified Immigration Desk); in the case of children minors, consent to the issue of the visa signed by the other parent (legalised consent)
- Documentation proving the status of family member or spouse of the foreign citizen residing in Italy, marriage certificate for the spouse, birth certificates for the children, all in original birth certificates for the children, all in original; they must then be translated and then legalized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Ramallah
- Travel ticket reservation
- Visa fee to be paid in cash
IMPORTANT: In order to obtain a reunification visa for the parents, it is necessary to prove and adequately document (e.g. with family status certificates, family status certificates duly translated and legalised by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs) that:
- the elderly parents do not have any other son/daughter residing in the country of origin
Once in Italy, they must register themselves with the local Anagrafe (Registry Office) and apply for a residence card at the relevant Questura within 8 working days from their arrival.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – Family Reunion > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family reunion visas are visas intended to reunite family members specifically husbands/wives, children and parents economically dependent on the sponsors. It is a long term (type D) period visa. The Family reunion visas must be authorized through the specified authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authorization in which are listed the family members who may apply for the visa.
Please be informed that your Nulla Osta is valid for 6 months from the date of issue. Your application will be accepted only with a valid Nulla Osta.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family reunion visas are visas intended to reunite family members specifically husbands/wives, children and parents economically dependent on the sponsors. It is a long term (type D) period visa. The Family reunion visas must be authorized through the specified authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authorization in which are listed the family members who may apply for the visa.
Please be informed that your Nulla Osta is valid for 6 months from the date of issue. Your application will be accepted only with a valid Nulla Osta.

## Visa Fees

[ita > pse > en > Table:Visa Type D – Family Reunion > Visa Fees 1]
Important Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Application form visa (type D) duly completed and signed by the visa applicant
- Recent photograph in passport size (2 per person)
- Valid passport or travel document (original plus one photocopy), valid at least 3 months and with at least two blank pages. The passport will be kept and returned with the visa, if approved;
- Nulla Osta” (Entry Clearance) issued no more than 6 months earlier by the competent “Sportello Unico” (Unified Immigration Desk); in the case of children minors, consent to the issue of the visa signed by the other parent (legalised consent)
- Documentation proving the status of family member or spouse of the foreign citizen residing in Italy, marriage certificate for the spouse, birth certificates for the children, all in original birth certificates for the children, all in original; they must then be translated and then legalized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Ramallah
- Travel ticket reservation
- Visa fee to be paid in cash
IMPORTANT: In order to obtain a reunification visa for the parents, it is necessary to prove and adequately document (e.g. with family status certificates, family status certificates duly translated and legalised by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs) that:
- the elderly parents do not have any other son/daughter residing in the country of origin
Once in Italy, they must register themselves with the local Anagrafe (Registry Office) and apply for a residence card at the relevant Questura within 8 working days from their arrival.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
The photo must fulfil the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Photo must have plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented does not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is around 15 calendar days. Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
